microsoft_ft: **Owned end-to-end technical delivery** for enterprise accounts at the scale of Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp — configuring, integrating, and shipping resilience solutions without handoffs, directly mirroring the forward-deployed ownership model Brellium requires.

Built and deployed an internal **Resilience Automation Platform** from 0→1, translating enterprise customer requirements into self-service configurations that reduced operational toil by 30% and scaled delivery across multiple concurrent accounts simultaneously.

Served as the **on-site technical partner** bridging Customer Success and Engineering — joining executive-level account calls, translating business needs into technical plans in real time, and driving workstreams to completion without CS chasing engineering for updates.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, shipping a **custom drill program in 4 months** with a 94% recovery rate by designing integration patterns, surfacing critical hardware defects, and aligning cross-org stakeholders under tight delivery timelines.

Acted as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating directly with customer IT teams to manage integration complexity, scope technical requirements, and ensure delivery with zero escalations.

Scaled an AI agent and restructured planning workflows to cut cycle time by **39%** and grow drill capacity by 21% — applying LLM-powered automation to reduce manual overhead and increase throughput across a portfolio of 14+ enterprise accounts.

Flagged recurring customer configuration gaps back to Product as platform improvement opportunities, **closing the CS-to-Engineering feedback loop** and accelerating roadmap prioritization for features that scaled across all enterprise accounts.
microsoft_2023: Drove **enterprise adoption of AI-powered workflows** by conducting hands-on demos and technical onboarding sessions across 14 Azure service teams, translating complex AI code-generation capabilities into practical configurations that saved 37 engineering hours monthly.

Shaped the product roadmap by running **11+ structured discovery interviews** with enterprise engineering teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and securing stakeholder alignment to add intent-based YAML generation — a direct output of field-learned customer requirements.

Accelerated technical delivery for internal customers by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search platform**, enforcing metadata standards and integration patterns that cut lookup time by 83% across distributed engineering teams.
microsoft_2022: **Generated $3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional buy-in across 140+ teams on a unified automation prioritization framework — coordinating with customer-side IT and engineering stakeholders to drive end-to-end execution.

Conducted deep-dive discovery with 20+ enterprise service teams to surface **81 hours of per-region manual toil**, then built a technical roadmap to automate critical deployment paths and eliminate recurring operational bottlenecks at scale.

Engineered a **Power BI integration dashboard** tracking automation coverage across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify delivery gaps and prioritize high-impact workstreams — a direct analog to tracking technical workstreams across multiple enterprise accounts.
amazon_robotics: Delivered a **zero-downtime OS migration** across a 2,000+ unit pilot fleet by owning the end-to-end technical plan — mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, coordinating with IT and Operations, and shipping the solution without escalation or missed milestones.

Partnered directly with Engineering and Operations teams to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and eliminating manual handoffs that had slowed delivery across cross-functional stakeholders.

Resolved **40+ high-priority integration tickets** by owning sprint backlog prioritization and Agile ceremonies, keeping multiple technical workstreams moving simultaneously and stabilizing the developer intake process under fast-paced startup-like delivery pressure.
pro_painters: **Increased job bookings by 26%** by owning the full technical and operational delivery of a new CRM configuration — designing the end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflow for 200+ monthly proposals and driving adoption without external engineering support.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversion rates by 2.7% by independently deploying a digital-first go-to-market strategy, reconfiguring the website and Google Business profile to align messaging with target customer needs.

Identified and closed margin leaks by conducting a **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, translating raw operational data into pricing model recommendations that directly improved leadership decision-making.

skills_priority: Forward-Deployed Engineering: end-to-end technical delivery, enterprise integrations, custom configuration
Customer-Facing Technical Partnership: requirements gathering, real-time solutioning, CS-Engineering bridge
Software Engineering: Python, SQL, APIs, CI/CD, YAML, distributed systems, Azure
AI/ML-Powered Products: AI agents, LLM-powered automation, semantic search, RAG, prompt engineering
Enterprise Account Ownership: multi-account delivery, IT coordination, integration patterns
Program Execution: cross-functional alignment, Agile/Scrum, roadmap feedback loops, OKRs
Tooling & Platforms: Power BI, Copilot Studio, data pipelines, workflow automation
Coursework: Data Structures, Algorithms, Databases, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning
tailoring_notes: 'End-to-end technical delivery without handoffs' (JD: 'ship the technical solution, end to end — no handoffs') woven into microsoft_ft bullet 1 and amazon_robotics bullet 1 to mirror Brellium's ownership model explicitly.

'CS-to-Engineering feedback loop' and 'bridge' language (JD: 'Sit between Customer Success and Engineering… flag it back to Product as a gap') surfaced in microsoft_ft bullets 3 and 7, directly naming the bridging function Brellium describes.

'Enterprise integration patterns' and 'customer IT teams' (JD Bonus: 'coordinating with customer IT teams') used in microsoft_ft bullet 5 (sovereign-cloud test) and amazon_robotics bullet 1 to match the integration coordination language from the JD.
'Multiple accounts simultaneously' (JD: 'manage multiple accounts simultaneously') addressed explicitly in microsoft_ft bullet 2 and bullet 6 to show Cyrus has operated at portfolio scale.

'Field-learned customer requirements → roadmap feedback' (JD: 'when you see the same customer need come up twice, flag it back to Product') directly instantiated in microsoft_2023 bullet 2 (11 discovery interviews driving roadmap change) and microsoft_ft bullet 7.
